scope.summary: Implement per-garment UV atlas isolation so the UV editor panel and tailor_uv_inspect filter islands to the operator- or model-selected garment_id only
acceptance_criteria: The UV editor panel renders only the UV islands of the garment currently selected in the garment list; islands from other garments in the same workspace are hidden
The tailor_uv_inspect MCP tool accepts an optional garment_id filter and returns only islands for that garment when the filter is set
Switching garment selection in the garment list updates the UV editor panel without a full page reload
When no garment is selected, the UV editor panel displays an empty state with a prompt to select a garment
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: capture UV editor with two garments in workspace; assert only the selected garment islands are visible
scope.risk_if_missed: In multi-garment scenes (outfit with top, skirt, accessory), the UV editor atlas becomes unreadable. Operators cannot diagnose per-garment texture issues. Models cannot programmatically isolate UV state for a single garment layer.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
